data.title: Sanofi sued by Texas AG for running kickback scheme for doctors
data.description:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton ((R)) has filed a lawsuit against Sanofi (SNY), accusing the French pharma giant of illegally running a kickback scheme aimed at encouraging doctors to prescribe its medicines.

By providing healthcare providers with nurse services and insurance support services as part of its "Free Nurse Program” and “Support Services Program," the company influenced the kind of drugs Texans received, according to the lawsuit.

With some of Sanofi’s (SNY) drugs used to treat chronic ailments, the scheme ensured that Sanofi (SNY) received years-long sources of revenue even though the drugs were not the most appropriate course of therapy for the patients.

The kickbacks violated the Texas Health Care Program Fraud Prevention Act (“THFPA”), Paxton said in a statement, adding that he is seeking monetary relief of over $1M, including civil penalties, as well as an injunction to halt any further illegal acts.

The lawsuit marked Paxton’s latest attempt to challenge Big Pharma. In August, he sued Eli Lilly (LLY) over allegations that the Indiana-based pharma giant bribed medical providers to boost prescriptions for its weight loss therapy Zepbound and other drugs.

data.title: CME's latest 24/7 move may hasten an end to the closing bell
data.description:
There's a reason that Amazon just overtook longtime rival Walmart as the world's largest company by revenue. It's called "Amazon-ification." It refers to the shift in expectations toward 24/7 availability via a sprawling fulfillment network, with real-time data and frictionless interactions. Over in the investing world, traders have been demanding the same conditions, with market forces bracing for the coming "Amazon-ification."

Snapshot: CME Group (CME) is moving closer to round-the-clock trading, with crypto futures and options that will trade 24/7 on CME Globex starting on May 29. The move comes as the Chicago-based company witnesses record volumes of crypto futures and options. Year-to-date, the average daily volume was up 46% year-over-year to 407,200 contracts, and average daily open interest increased 7% to 335,400 contracts.

It's going mainstream. The retail crowd has been trading overnight for some time through alternative trading systems like Blue Ocean, which was popularized on Robinhood (HOOD), Webull, and Interactive Brokers (IBKR). Exchanges have also jumped on the all-day trading bandwagon, with the launch of the 24X National Exchange, the New York Stock Exchange (ICE) extending Arca trading to 22 hours per day, and the Nasdaq (NDAQ) recently filing a proposal to move to a 23/5 schedule.

About the infrastructure: Similar to Amazon, which requires a massive logistics network, financial institutions need the required plumbing to make these features available to the masses. The National Securities Clearing Corporation is scheduled to move to a 24/5 operating schedule in June, which would result in the immediate guarantee of overnight trades and reduce risk for brokerages. The Securities Information Processor, or SIP system, which consolidates and broadcasts stock prices, is also in the middle of transitioning to 24-hour data dissemination from Sunday to Friday night.

data.title: WillScot targets $25M–$30M annual cost savings through network optimization as modular orders rise 17%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: WillScot Holdings Corporation (WSC) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Timothy Boswell, President and CEO, described the company as “emerging from a period of rapid transformation with an opportunity to set a new standard of performance in our industry through focused execution of our strategy.” He highlighted stabilization in the modular lease portfolio, noting, “Revenue from modular products was effectively flat year-over-year. So the lease portfolio is stabilizing as a result of our initiatives despite the continued contraction of nonresidential square footage starts in Q4.”
  • Boswell emphasized the company’s top priority as “returning the business to steady organic growth,” with a path to “positive organic revenue growth inflection in the second half of the year.” He underscored momentum in enterprise accounts, stating, “Enterprise account revenue was up 7% year-over-year for the full year in 2025 and up 10% year-over-year in Q4 excluding one large seasonal container customer.”
  • Three operational initiatives were detailed: network optimization to exit surplus real estate and idle fleet, rollout of an enhanced scheduling and route optimization platform, and improvements in support center operations for cash collections and customer service.
  • Matthew Jacobsen, Executive VP and CFO, reported, “In the fourth quarter, total revenue was $566 million and adjusted EBITDA was $250 million representing a margin of 44.2%.” Jacobsen also introduced the network optimization plan, stating, “As leases expire over the next 4 years, and we exit approximately 25% of our leased acreage, we expect to realize between $25 million and $30 million of annual real estate cost savings.”

Outlook

  • The 2026 outlook calls for revenue of approximately $2.175 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $900 million. Jacobsen described the guidance as “a conservative view relative to our current run rate beginning the year and does not include benefits from ongoing internal initiatives that, if sustained, could drive year-over-year leasing revenue growth at some point in the second half of the year, and place us on a growth trajectory into 2027.”
  • First quarter 2026 guidance includes “approximately $515 million of revenue for the first quarter and adjusted EBITDA of approximately $200 million.”
  • CapEx for 2026 is guided at $275 million, with investments focused on differentiated product categories.
  • Management stated internal plans and compensation targets are set above the official outlook, reflecting confidence in potential upside if commercial momentum continues.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter total revenue was $566 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $250 million and a 44.2% margin.
  • Full year 2025 total revenue was $2.28 billion and adjusted EBITDA was $971 million at a margin of 42.6%.
  • Adjusted free cash flow for the quarter was $91 million, and for the full year totaled $489 million, exceeding guidance.
  • A noncash restructuring charge of $302 million was recognized related to accelerated depreciation on rental equipment as part of the network optimization plan.
  • Net CapEx for the year was $273 million, up 17% compared to 2024.
  • The company returned $151 million to shareholders via repurchases and dividends in 2025.

Q&A

  • Andrew Wittmann, Baird, asked about sustainability of the order book momentum. Boswell responded that larger RFP wins in enterprise accounts are a significant factor, “data center activity, specifically in contractual written revenue, we expect that subvertical could be up 50% year-over-year in 2026.”
  • Wittmann followed up on VAPS momentum. Boswell said, “penetration levels... are slightly increasing,” attributing this mainly to a mix shift rather than per-unit penetration improvement, but noted, “fencing and perimeter solutions is set for a nationwide rollout this year.”
  • Angel Castillo, Morgan Stanley, probed for details on free cash flow guidance. Jacobsen answered, “our math would say around $415 million of adjusted free cash flow,” explaining that this excludes $35 million in network optimization implementation costs.
  • Steven Ramsey, Thompson Research Group, inquired about enterprise growth drivers. Boswell clarified, “the growth in that segment is volume-driven, and that’s a function of going deeper with customers... as well as the vertical business development strategy.”
  • Kyle Menges, Citigroup, asked about portable storage rates. Boswell said, “as reported average rate up 9% year-over-year... is almost entirely mix driven by rapid growth within our cold storage offering.”
  • Menges also asked about AI initiatives. Boswell described several AI-enabled tools in sales and pricing, but added, “there’s still a lot of basic blocking and tackling in the back office, where we’re seeing traction on collections and customer service.”
  • John Ronan Kennedy, Barclays, focused on conservative guidance and volume/price assumptions. Jacobsen reiterated, “the guide that we’ve provided is kind of a continuation of recent trends,” with pricing and volume pressures mainly in storage.
  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank, questioned inclusion of a potential revenue inflection in guidance. Jacobsen confirmed, “our conservative guide is based on the run rate coming out of last year,” with any improvement seen as upside.
  • Philip Ng, Jefferies, asked about lag between nonresidential starts and units on rent. Boswell replied, “we’re seeing meaningful activation and order growth in a declining starts environment. And that’s a bit unusual in our business.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally cautious, repeatedly pressing on the sustainability of order trends, drivers of guidance conservatism, and the visibility of a potential revenue inflection. Their tone was neutral to slightly negative, especially in seeking clarity on the sustainability of recent momentum and the practical impact of strategic initiatives.
  • Management maintained a confident yet measured tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing “conservative” guidance and upside potential if recent trends hold. In Q&A, Boswell displayed cautious optimism but avoided overcommitting, especially around inflection timing: “it’s a little too soon to extrapolate all of that across the rest of the year.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained disciplined and focused on execution, with increased transparency about conservatism in forecasts. Analysts’ skepticism persisted quarter-over-quarter, with a notable focus on validating early positive signals.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is consistent with the “conservative” approach established in Q3, with explicit statements that internal targets are set higher.
  • Strategic focus on network optimization has advanced, with the plan approved and a significant noncash charge recognized in Q4. The previous quarter’s discussion was more tentative, with the plan under board review.
  • Management transition to Boswell as CEO was completed, with further alignment of the sales organization and operational priorities.
  • Modular order book strength is now more pronounced, with a 17% year-over-year increase in pending orders, compared to flat order trends and focus on stabilization in the prior quarter.
  • Analysts’ questions continued to revolve around sustainability of new business momentum, the impact of network optimization, and the timing of a return to growth, mirroring the themes from Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing volume pressures in the traditional storage business, identifying a $50 million revenue headwind entering 2026.
  • The network optimization initiative will result in $60 million of cash costs over several years, with $35 million anticipated in 2026.
  • Guidance remains intentionally conservative, not extrapolating early positive order trends into the second half of the year.
  • Analysts highlighted risks around order momentum sustainability, the lag between nonresidential starts and activations, and the impact of large project timing.
  • Management’s mitigation strategies include deepening enterprise account penetration, enhanced local sales execution, and operational improvements to drive margin expansion once growth resumes.

Final Takeaway

WillScot Holdings Corporation enters 2026 with a stabilized modular lease portfolio and a robust order book, supported by a conservative financial outlook and a disciplined operational strategy. The company is advancing a multiyear network optimization plan projected to deliver $25 million to $30 million in annual cost savings, while focusing on operational readiness and commercial execution to drive a potential return to organic growth in the second half of the year. Management’s alignment around these priorities and cautious approach to guidance reflect a commitment to delivering consistent results and long-term value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Brainstorm Cell secures $1M strategic financing
data.description:
Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics (BCLI) has entered into a definitive agreement for a strategic private placement of $1M.

The common stock was priced at $0.60 per share, representing a significant premium to the $0.54 closing price on February 9, 2026, the date the transaction was finalized. 

The financing is being completed in two tranches, with the first payment of $500,000 already received and the final payment of $500,000 scheduled for receipt within 30 days. It follows a period of intense strategic activity for BrainStorm, as the company finalizes its Phase 3b trial design under a special protocol assessment and engages in high-level discussions regarding the pending citizens petition for accelerated approval.

Proceeds from the financing will be used to support ongoing regulatory initiatives, prepayments for the upcoming Phase 3b trial of the firm’s investigational ALS treatment NurOwn, and general corporate purposes.

The investment also includes 120% warrant coverage for five years with an exercise price of $1.00 per share.

data.title: Bimergen Energy prices $13.6M equity offering
data.description:
Bimergen Energy (BESS) announced it has priced an underwritten public offering at $4.00 per share, raising $13.6M in gross proceeds before expenses.

The offering includes shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrants in lieu thereof) along with one accompanying warrant per common stock or pre-funded warrant. Each warrant is exercisable for one share of common stock and is valid for five years from the date of issuance.

Bimergen (BESS) also gave underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to 200,000 additional shares (or pre-funded warrants) and/or an additional 200,000 warrants to cover over-allotments, if any.

The company said it plans to use the proceeds to fund battery energy storage system projects and for general working capital needs.

The shares of common stock and warrants are expected to begin trading on the NYSE American on February 20, 2026, under the symbols “BESS” and “BESSWS”, respectively. The offering is expected to close on February 23, 2026, subject to satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

data.title: Moderna sued for violating patent linked to Pfizer/ BioNTech COVID-19 shot
data.description:
Moderna (MRNA) faces a lawsuit in the U.S. for allegedly infringing patent rights related to the COVID-19 shot developed by Pfizer (PFE) and BioNTech (BNTX), marking the latest such case among COVID-19 vaccine makers.

German vaccine maker BioNTech (BNTX) sued the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech company on Thursday, alleging that Moderna’s (MRNA) COVID-19 vaccine, mNEXSPIKE, violates a patent related to its rival shot, Comirnaty.

The lawsuit filed in Delaware federal court claimed that mNEXSPIKE, which was approved by the U.S. FDA in 2025, infringed upon BioNTech’s (BNTX) Patent No. 12,133,899.

The patent in question covers a streamlined messenger RNA-based vaccine design that BioNTech (BNTX) claimed it discovered while developing Comirnaty with its partner Pfizer (PFE). However, the New York-based pharma giant is not directly involved in the case.

The messenger RNA-based vaccine is expected to account for 55% of Moderna's (MRNA) vaccine revenue during the 2025/26 respiratory virus season, according to the suit.

A Moderna (MRNA) spokesperson told Bloomberg that it is aware of the lawsuit and “will defend ourselves against these claims.” BioNTech (BNTX) wasn’t immediately available for requests for comments.

The lawsuit marked the latest patent row among the COVID-19 vaccine makers. Moderna (MRNA) filed lawsuits against Pfizer (PFE) and BioNTech (BNTX) in 2022, claiming their COVID-19 vaccine infringed upon its patent rights.
